data.short_description: Story Circle beat sheet for the Novel Studio app: Dan Harmon's 8-step circle as an adoptable template. The steps live in this entity's lore; a novel adopts the sheet in its Blueprint, and its scenes' beat property uses these step names exactly.

lore.title: Beats
lore.content:
The 8 steps of Dan Harmon's Story Circle (the monomyth compressed to a wheel), in story order. Each step is roughly an eighth of the story — the circle bends freely. Steps 1–4 descend from the ordered, familiar world into chaos; steps 5–8 climb back out, changed. Scenes' beat property must use these names exactly as spelled.

You (step 1) — a character in their zone of comfort: who they are when nothing is asking anything of them. The familiar world the circle will leave and return to.

Need (step 2) — but they want something. A lack, hunger, or disturbance makes staying put impossible; the want may be shallow now — the story will reveal what's under it.

Go (step 3) — so they enter an unfamiliar situation. The threshold is crossed: a new world, a new role, a new game with rules they don't yet know.

Search (step 4) — to which they have to adapt. Trial, error, and cost: the character flails, learns the new rules, and pays for each lesson. The deepest point of the descent.

Find (step 5) — until they get what they wanted. The goal is reached, the prize is in hand — and it is rarely shaped the way they imagined it.

Take (step 6) — but they pay a heavy price for it. The prize's true cost lands: a loss, a sacrifice, a revelation that the want and the need were never the same thing.

Return (step 7) — and go back to where they started. The road home, carrying what was found and what it cost; the familiar world seen with new eyes.

Change (step 8) — now capable of change. The circle closes where it opened, but the character is not who they were in step 1 — the story's proof, on the page, of what the journey did.
